data.claim: Random Forest substantially outperforms logistic regression for predicting civil war onset.

data.name: Imputation-before-split leakage
data.reason: Common failure mode; starting here because the repo has explicit imputation in prepare_data.

data.name: Feature engineering mismatch with paper
data.reason: Repo constructs several derived features not documented in the paper's methods section.

data.name: Patient/entity split missing
data.reason: Country-year panel: if country IDs leak across splits, years from the same country inflate performance.

data.name: Metric implementation bug
data.reason: Repo's eval uses a custom AUC — worth checking whether it matches sklearn.

data.result: Imputer fit on full df. Weak evidence alone; could also be patient split or feature-engineering issue contributing.

data.description: Check whether country_id appears in both train and test.

data.result: country_id appears in both splits. But this is expected for a country-year panel where observations are assumed iid within country. Neither strongly supports nor refutes.

data.detail: Reached 30-turn cap without any single hypothesis exceeding the 0.85 confidence threshold. Best candidate remains H1 at 0.72. Recommend a narrower Quick Check to disambiguate H1 vs H2.
